title: actinella tweediei
side_effects: Actinella tweediei can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, which can be caused by an allergic reaction or an overdose of its toxic compounds. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant may also lead to more severe side effects, including liver damage and kidney failure. In some cases, the plant's toxic alkaloids can cause neurological symptoms like dizziness, confusion, and even seizures. If not handled properly, Actinella tweediei can pose a significant risk to human health, emphasizing the need for caution and informed use.
